params.name: Under a Pipal Tree
params.description:
On Sep 8, 660 BC, Siddhartha began to meditate under a pipal tree in Bodh Gaya.
Siddhartha, the son of the king of the Shakya clan in Nepal, was prophesied by a sage to become a great king. However, after four excursions outside his palace, Siddhartha witnessed the suffering of birth, aging, sickness, and death, and decided to renounce his throne in search of liberation. He left his homeland and practiced asceticism for a long time, but realized that extreme mortification did not lead to true …
